nodes.label: Mock HTTP Status Pattern
nodes.rationale: Allows BFF (Grupo 1) to test error handling without triggering real failures. Controlled by USE_MOCKS env var + X-USE-MOCKS header double-gate to prevent accidental activation in production.

nodes.label: Batch Idempotency Pattern
nodes.rationale: BatchJob table stores idempotency_key as PK; on duplicate key the existing job is returned without re-queuing. Prevents duplicate batch recalculations from client retries.

nodes.label: Real-time + Batch dual ingestion for resilience
nodes.norm_label: real-time + batch dual ingestion for resilience

nodes.label: Idempotent shipment delivery log (upsert by shipment_id)
nodes.norm_label: idempotent shipment delivery log (upsert by shipment_id)

nodes.label: Exponential Backoff retries on Pub/Sub handlers (max 5)
nodes.norm_label: exponential backoff retries on pub/sub handlers (max 5)

nodes.label: Double Gate Mock Activation Pattern
nodes.contributor: rationale: USE_MOCKS env AND X-USE-MOCKS header both required — prevents accidental mock data reaching real clients even when server is in mock mode

nodes.label: CQRS Write/Read Path Separation Pattern
nodes.contributor: rationale: writes are async via Pub/Sub workers, reads are sync HTTP — separating allows independent scaling and matches async event-driven ingestion with synchronous analytics queries

nodes.label: Idempotency Key Pattern for Batch Endpoint
nodes.contributor: rationale: Idempotency-Key UUID on POST /reports/batch/recalculate stored in batch_jobs table — prevents duplicate batch executions; second request with same key returns 200 with original job_id instead of re-queuing
nodes.norm_label: idempotency key pattern for batch endpoint

nodes.label: Pub/Sub Ack/Nack Error Handling Strategy
nodes.contributor: rationale: callback acks on success or unknown event type, nacks only on exception/invalid JSON — combined with tenacity retry (5 attempts exponential backoff) and DLQ on GCP to avoid permanent message loss
